info.title: External Service
info.description: Describes the operations that needs to be implemented at the external system to receive notifications for subscriptions

400.description:
Bad Request

The event will not be resent and an investigation is necessary to see why the service did not like the request.

401.description:
Unauthorized

The event will not be resent and an investigation is necessary to see why T-ONE is not authenitcating itself correctly.

403.description:
Forbidden

The event will not be resent and an investigation is necessary to see why T-ONE does not have access to the endpoint.

500.description:
Internal Server Error

The event will not be resent and an investigation is necessary to see why the service did not successfully process the request.

503.description:
Service Unavailable

The event will be resent after a short delay.

post.description: Each event published from T-ONE will be sent to this endpoint.

index.description: Subscription unique sequential counter intended for the subscriber to detect missed messages.

eventType.description: The possible event types are described in the specification. An unknown event type should be accepted and ignored by the external service.

eventData.description: Contains information about the event. Most event data contains an ID to be able to recognize the resource the event is all about.

basic.description: T-ONE can authenticate itself with basic authentication if needed, but it's completely optional. The external service should include the basic authentication settings necessary when creating a subscription.

traceparent.name: traceparent
traceparent.description: The traceparent header represents the incoming request in a tracing system in a common format, W3C Trace Context
